observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Accountant to submit the documents containing adequate information as basis for the recording of the Other Investment Account.
observations.managementAction: Documents cannot be located

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office strictly observe compliance with the perpetual inventory method of accounting cited above for the supplies and materials procured for construction projects implemented by administration.  Also, require the Municipal Treasurer, tasked to enforce controls on the receipt and utilization, to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) monthly to the Accounting Office.
We recommended and Management agreed to undertake all the procedures under COA Circular 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that concerned personnel of the Accounting and Treasury Units coordinate and regularly reconcile their records monthly or at least quarterly for an accurate and fair presentation of cash accounts in the financial statement.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned personnel to prepare the IIRUP and initiate proper disposal to avoid further deterioration of the properties.

We recommended and the Management agreed to enforce the deduction for retention or warranty security to adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, not only in infrastructure projects but also in the procurement of goods.  Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with at least 1% but not more than 5% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount.

We recommend that the Management strictly adhere to rules and regulations on procurement, properly plan and monitor the procurement process, including its timelines, provide additional manpower to the Office of the Municipal Procurement Officer, and refrain from the practice of withdrawing the supplies and materials in advance from the known supplier before procurement documents will be prepared.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers relating to the final payment of infrastructure projects are adequately supported with mandatory documentary requirements before processing the payments.

We recommended that Management instruct the MHO to prepare and submit the following documents to support their claims of ADHP and SRA:
List of the eligible HWs determined by the Local Health board;
DTRs/accomplishment report; and
Approved working arrangement indicating the area of assignment.
Also, advise Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation as provided by Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly impose the collectors' timely submission of their reports to submit timely the RCD to the Accounting Office to ensure the on-time remittance of barangays' shares from RPT as provided under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the Management request HDMF to consider the excess amount as advance payment of GS in future remittance; and strengthen the internal control system, particularly the review of remittances on statutory obligations, to prevent from recurrence of the deficiency.

We recommended that Management prepare budgets based on the duly approved Annual Investment Plan indicating each program, project and activity and identify the expected outputs for each PPA.  It shall be presented in detail, stating the facts of the projects, including the exact location.  For appropriations already presented in a lump sum or in generic terms, secured a separate SB Resolution to serve as an authority to enter into a contract on behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance with Section 22 (c) of R.A. 7160 and pertinent rulings of G.R. No. 175527 dated December 8, 2008.

We recommended that Management prepare monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRMMF in its prescribed format as duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provision stated under Budget Circular No. 2020-01 in granting COVID 19 Hazard Pay and submit the authority and/or schedule/work arrangement of the employees required to physically report for work during MECQ as approved by their Heads of Offices.
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: Accounting unit prepared the adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the entry to record the receivables in the books of LEE-Water System.

Further, direct the Municipal Economic Enterprise Office to strengthen the collections and strictly impose Section 38, item 5 of the Cagwait Municipal Economic Enterprise Code to facilitate the collection of its outstanding receivables.
observations.managementAction: Record the Receivables and facilitate the collection of outstanding receivables

observations.recommendation: Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that only half or fifty percent of the negotiated price of the amount of land will be paid upon the execution of the deed of sale, and the remaining half will be paid upon the transfer of title in the name of the LGU, as provided under Section 243 of GAM for LGUs.                                                                                     Further, direct the Municipal Assessor to apprise the audit team of the recently transferred lots in the name of the LGU. Finally, instruct the Municipal Accountant that all disbursement vouchers relating to land purchase are properly supported with necessary documents before processing the payments.
observations.managementAction: On the process of transferring lots in the name of LGU

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management strictly observe the provisions of DBM Budget Circular 2020-02 and any subsequent Circulars relating to the grants of incentives to ensure rightful claims of qualified personnel.
observations.managementAction: Observed DBM Budget Circular

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management be updated with the new guidelines that are necessary for the operations, especially during the Emergency Phase. Prepare and issue Acknowledgement Receipt for In-Kind Donations to all future receipts to acknowledge the donations and proper accounting and recording. Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any proof of receipt must be prepared to prove the distribution to the intended recipient. And monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations for all in-kind donations by a private entity in compliance with COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 and strictly follow the provisions thereto.

We recommended that Management direct LDRRM Officer to be updated with the new issuance related to DRRM policies and guidelines. Prepare and submit the one-time report of the donations received, distributed and balances to the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Auditor's office as required in COA Circular 2020-009.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to fairly present the current and non-current portion of its outstanding Loans Payable and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary loan details to provide users of fairly presented financial statements.

We recommended the Management to: 

a.	Use the required format of a sequentially numbered trip ticket duly approved by the Agency Head or the authorized representative for proper monitoring of fuel consumption with complete information being filled out in every detail needed to support the claims for payment of fuel expenses. 

b.	Enforce the "No Driver's Trip Ticket, No Withdrawal Policy." 

c.	Direct the authorized personnel to reconcile every month the driver's trip ticket with the Monthly Fuel consumption and Summary of Monthly Report of official Travels as mandated under COA Circular No. 77-61 D.6 to determine whether the said fuel usage is valid and is being used appropriately.

d.	Install controls on the monitoring of issuance of the fuel and strictly adhere to the guidelines on the use of government vehicles.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification on the above audit finding. Direct the responsible personnel to diligently process and observe BIR's policies on the payment of taxes to avoid surcharges, interests, and compromised penalties. In case of delay, any penalty and surcharges are charged personally to officials or employees directly responsible.

We recommended the management to submit justification on the excess of the Contract Cost to the COA Evaluated Cost in the amount of P395,507.49 for further evaluation. We also recommended Management to ensure compliance with the existing rules and regulations in the preparation of Approved Budget cost (ABC) and that costs estimates be in accordance with the project’s plans and specifications.
observations.managementAction: Justification Letter to COA

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to conform to all the agreements embodied in the MOA entered into by and between the Source of Agency and the Implementing Agency. In Addition, identify the factors that caused the delay in the implementation of the projects and see to it to have immediate remedial actions to address those factors.

We recommended   that the Municipal Treasurer were  not ensure  accurate  assessment  of business taxes by the following  the proper  procedures  as specified  in the Local Treasury  Operations  (LTOM) and by  checking books  of accounts  and /or  ledger of business establishment  with corresponding  Sworn Statement  of Gross Receipts/ Sales  and Income  Tax Return  in accordance  to Items (d) & (e)  of Section 2C. 04 of the Municipal Revised Code of 2016.  . Moreover, correct the erroneous interpretation in the computation of taxes based on the tax table.
observations.managementAction: MTO properly assessed the Code of Accounts of Business establishments

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to render an accounting on the result of recently conducted physical inventory and reconcile differences of the two records.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of accounting records of physical inventory is on going

observations.recommendation:
We recommend directing the following personnel/ Offices:

Inventory Committee:

Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment.

Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type showing the balance of property and equipment per cards and per count and the shortage/overage, if any.

Prepare the report yearly in three copies, certified correct by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency, and submit to the Office of the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.  For CY 2014, submit the report on or before March 31, 2015.

Accounting Office:	
Reconcile the report of the inventory committee with the control accounts in the general ledger.

Determine the discrepancy and effect adjustments in the books, if warranted.

Maintain and update regularly the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC)

Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer:

Prepare the inventory guidelines, indicating the date of physical inventory, who is to supervise the count, the personnel to act as counters and checkers, and the sequence of the counting, for approval by the Municipal Mayor.

Maintain and update regularly the Property/Equipment Cards for each class of fixed asset.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the inventory committee to conduct physical count of PPE and required the Municipal Accountant to  reconcile the records
observations.reason: Physical Count of PPE and reconciliation of records by the accounting and treasury is on going.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting cash advances to officials and employees having unliquidated cash advances. Moreover, direct him to submit monitoring report of unliquidated cash advances as of end of the first quarter of 2015 for the audit team to issue order of suspension of salaries of erring accountable officers as prescribed under COA Circular 2012-004.  Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 is a must to avoid accumulation of unsettled accounts.
observations.reason: There are few officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances who were granted with cash advances as shown in the monitoring report
